pi_items.source: Task setup script copies TitanicSurvival.csv to this path.
Task setup script explicitly points to this expected path for the generated OMV file.
Jamovi uses the 'propTestN' module for N Outcomes (Chi-Square Goodness of Fit), consistent with verifier.py analysis type checks.
The task instructions target this specific variable, which is present in TitanicSurvival.csv and has 3 distinct levels.
Exact Chi-Square value computed from TitanicSurvival.csv is 258.01, which conforms to this minimum boundary of 200.
Exact Chi-Square value computed from TitanicSurvival.csv is 258.01, which conforms to this maximum boundary of 320.
The passengerClass variable has 3 groups (1st, 2nd, 3rd), which equates to degrees of freedom = 3 - 1 = 2.

privileged_info_summary: The dataset is TitanicSurvival from the carData R package. It contains 1,309 records with no missing values in the target variable 'passengerClass'. The test evaluates if passenger classes are equally distributed across 1st, 2nd, and 3rd classes. A Chi-Square Goodness of Fit test (propTestN) against equal expected frequencies (436.33) yields a significant exact Chi-Square statistic of 258.01 with 2 degrees of freedom (p < .001), indicating the 3rd class (n=709) is overrepresented compared to 1st (n=323) and 2nd (n=277) classes. This result perfectly aligns with all metadata bounds and assertions.
